title: About Commercial Roofers of Shreveport | Commercial Roofing in Shreveport, LA
metaDescription: Commercial roofing across Shreveport-Bossier: re-roofing, restoration, and repair for casinos, medical campuses, I-20 industrial, and parish buildings.

internalLinks.anchor: Silicone Coatings: Buying Years Back on an Aging Roof
Oil City: Roofing the Buildings Left Behind by an Early Oil Boom
The Gaming Floor Can't Close, So the Roof Above It Doesn't Get a Slow Season

html:
Roofers Who Talk Work Orders, Not Sales Pitches

Commercial Roofers of Shreveport works on the roofs behind Shreveport-Bossier's older commercial buildings: manufacturing plants off the rail spurs, casino properties along the riverfront, medical offices in the Ochsner LSU Health corridor, and the distribution warehouses stacked along I-20. When you call us, you get a straight answer about what's failing on your roof, what it costs to fix now versus defer to next budget cycle, and how we keep your building operating while we're on top of it.

How We Fit Into Your Work Order Queue

You've got tenants to answer to, production lines that can't stop for a half-day, or a casino floor that runs around the clock. We build our schedule around yours, not the other way. That means night or early-morning mobilization when a facility can't tolerate daytime noise over a food court or a patient wing, and it means staging materials so a crew isn't blocking a loading dock during a shift change.

We document everything as we go: photos before and after each section, a punch list at close-out, and a written scope that matches what actually got done. If you report to a regional facilities director or a property management company, that paperwork is what gets your invoice approved without a follow-up call.

The Building Stock We See Most in Shreveport-Bossier

A lot of the commercial roofs in this market are older than the businesses currently operating under them. Manufacturing buildings built in the 1970s and 80s still carry their original gravel-surfaced built-up roofs, patched in sections over the decades. Casino properties in Bossier City and downtown have grown through additions and renovations, so it's common to find three or four different roof systems tied together on one building, each aging at a different rate.

Medical office buildings along the Ochsner LSU Health corridor carry heavier rooftop mechanical loads than they did when they were built, which changes how a roof needs to drain and how much foot traffic it takes during service calls. Out along I-20, the distribution and warehouse buildings tend to run steel deck under single-ply or metal panel, with wide open field areas that make membrane condition easy to survey from the ground with a drone but hard to judge from a windshield.

What's Usually On the Work Order When We Get the Call

Most calls into our office fall into a handful of categories, roughly in order of how often they come in:

  • Active leak or water intrusion affecting tenant space, product, or equipment below
  • Roof condition inspection ahead of a lease renewal, insurance renewal, or capital budget cycle
  • Recover or full reroof scoping for a roof at or past its expected service life
  • Coating work to extend a roof's remaining years without a full tear-off
  • Metal panel repair or fastener replacement after a wind event
  • Drain, scupper, or edge metal repair tied to a ponding or overflow complaint

We treat all of these the same way: figure out what's actually happening on the roof before recommending anything, then lay out the options in terms you can take to whoever signs off on the spend.

Recover Versus Tear-Off — the Call We Walk Through With You

Because so much of Shreveport-Bossier's commercial stock is older, recover comes up constantly. A recover system installed over sound insulation and a dry deck can buy fifteen to twenty years at a fraction of tear-off cost, and it keeps your building watertight during the work instead of open to sky. But recover only makes sense if the existing roof is dry and structurally sound underneath, which is why we do a moisture survey before we ever quote a recover scope. If the deck is wet or the insulation has failed, recovering over it just traps the problem.

We'll show you both numbers — recover now versus tear-off now versus tear-off in three years after another round of patch repairs — so the decision fits your capital plan instead of our sales quota.

Weather That Actually Wears These Roofs Out

Shreveport-Bossier sits in a stretch of Louisiana that gets a real severe weather season each spring, with hail and straight-line wind events that split brittle membrane and knock loose flashing. Summer brings sustained high UV and humidity that break down older asphalt and rubber faster than manufacturer charts predict. Winter isn't long here, but the occasional ice event is enough to crack a membrane that's already lost its flexibility. All of that shows up first at seams, penetrations, and low spots where water sits — which is exactly where we start every inspection.

Questions Facilities Managers Ask Us

Do you work around tenant hours and production schedules?

Yes. Tell us your blackout windows — shift changes, patient hours, casino floor traffic, delivery schedules — and we build the mobilization plan around them.

Can you give us a roof condition report before we commit to a scope?

Yes, and we recommend it. A written inspection with photos and a moisture reading gives you something to bring to whoever approves the budget, whether that's a regional director or an owner.

Do you handle both flat membrane roofs and metal panel roofs?

Yes. Between the older BUR and single-ply stock downtown and the R-panel and standing seam buildings out along I-20, we work both regularly.

How do we know if our roof needs a recover or a full tear-off?

It comes down to what's under the surface. A moisture survey tells us whether the insulation and deck are still dry. If they are, recover is usually on the table. If not, we'll tell you why tear-off is the honest answer.

Do you provide documentation for insurance or lender requirements?

Yes. We can put together condition reports, scope documentation, and completion photos formatted for whatever your insurer, lender, or corporate office requires.
